text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill.
**DrJill:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** We all {wear|着る/身につける|/wɛr/|I wear a jacket today.} clothes, but we do not wear the same kind of clothes.
**DrJill:** We can wear {bright|明るい/鮮やか|/braɪt/|Bright colors make me happy.} colors.
**DrJill:** Red, yellow, purple.
**DrJill:** We can wear black and white.
**DrJill:** We can wear {dressy|フォーマルな/派手な|/ˈdresi/|Dressy clothes for a wedding.} clothes or {sporty|スポーティーな|/ˈspɔːrti/|Sporty clothes for exercise.} clothes.
**DrJill:** We also wear different clothes for different {places|場所|/ˈpleɪsɪz/|Different places have different rules.} and events.
**DrJill:** Like school or a wedding.
**DrJill:** What are you wearing today?
**DrJill:** That is our question for today.
**DrJill:** Now let's visit Anna.
**DrJill:** She answers the question.
**DrJill:** What are you wearing today?
**Anna:** Hi friends!
**Anna:** Tonight I am going to a {party|パーティー|/ˈpɑːrti/|We have a party tonight.}
**Anna:** So I am wearing my party clothes.
**Max:** You are talking about clothes.
**Max:** And our question for today is, what are you wearing today?
**Anna:** What am I wearing today?
**Anna:** This white coat is my party coat!
**Anna:** These red shoes are my party shoes.
**Anna:** Max, Daisy
**Anna:** You are {invited|招待された|/ɪnˈvaɪtɪd/|You are invited to my birthday.} to the party too!
**Anna:** I am invited, but I have nothing to wear.
**Max:** That is right, easy.
**Max:** You are a flower.
**Max:** Flowers do not wear clothes.
**Anna:** I want party clothes.
**Anna:** Do not {worry|心配しない}.
**Anna:** I can help.
**DrJill:** This {shirt|シャツ} is my favorite.
**DrJill:** What is your favorite {clothing|衣服/服}?
**DrJill:** Next, Anna {teaches|教える} us words to help us talk about clothes.
**Anna:** Let's talk about two words, this and these.
**Anna:** We use this to talk about one thing.
**Anna:** This shirt is {yellow|黄色}.
**Anna:** We use these to talk about two or more things.
**Anna:** These shirts are {orange|オレンジ色}.
**Anna:** What changes in these two sentences?
**Anna:** This shirt is yellow.
**Anna:** These shirts are orange.
**Anna:** This changed to these.
**Anna:** Is changed to R.
**Anna:** Shirt changed to shirts.
**Max:** And yellow changed to orange.
**DrJill:** Yes, Max.
**DrJill:** Thank you.
**DrJill:** Now let's {practice|練習する} the names of other types of clothes.
**Students:** This is a {skirt|スカート}.
**Students:** These are red skirts.
**Students:** These are blue {socks|靴下}.
**Students:** This is a pair of {shorts|半ズボン}.
**Students:** Here are two pairs of {pants|ズボン}.
**Students:** This is a black {coat|コート}.
**DrJill:** Great job!
**DrJill:** For {exercise|運動/エクササイズ}, I wear sporty clothes.
**DrJill:** For work, I wear work clothes.
**DrJill:** Now let's hear from some friends.
**DrJill:** They tell us what they {like|好む/好き|/laɪk/|I like pizza.} to {wear|着る/身につける|/wɛr/|I wear a blue shirt.}.
**Students:** This is my {favorite|お気に入りの|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite book.} t-shirt.
**Students:** My t-shirt is {dirty|汚れた|/ˈdɜːrti/|The floor is dirty.}, so I need to {change|変える/交換する|/tʃeɪndʒ/|Can I change my shoes?} it.
**Students:** Now it is clean.
**Students:** This is my {favorite|お気に入りの|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite toy.} dress.
**Students:** This is my {favorite|お気に入りの|/ˈfeɪvərɪt/|This is my favorite color.} skirt
**DrJill:** We can {buy|買う|/baɪ/|I buy a book.} clothes, but we can also {make|作る|/meɪk/|I make a cake.} clothes.
**DrJill:** So now let's go with Anna and {meet|会う/出会う|/miːt/|Let's meet at the park.} people who make clothes.
**Anna:** This is my friend Ruby.
**Anna:** She {makes|作る|/meɪks/|She makes cookies.} clothes.
**Anna:** She {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made a cake yesterday.} the {scarf|マフラー|/skɑːrf/|She wears a warm scarf.}.
**Anna:** And she {made|作った|/meɪd/|He made a painting.} this art.
**Students:** I {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made lunch.} this dress.
**Students:** I {made|作った|/meɪd/|She made a mistake.} this skirt.
**Students:** I {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made a cake.} these hats.
**Anna:** I am ready for the {party|パーティー|/ˈpɑːrti/|We have a party tonight.}.
**Anna:** Max, Daisy, you both look {great|素晴らしい|/greɪt/|That's a great idea.}
**Max:** I feel {great|素晴らしい|/greɪt/|I feel great today.}.
**Anna:** I feel {amazing|驚くべき/素晴らしい|/əˈmeɪzɪŋ/|The view is amazing.}.
**Anna:** My friend {made|作った|/meɪd/|I made dinner.} it for you, Daisy.
**Anna:** Come on.
**Anna:** Let's go to the {party|パーティー|/ˈpɑːrti/|We are going to a party.}!
**DrJill:** Now you can talk about your clothes.
**DrJill:** Use the words you learn here.
**DrJill:** And join us again next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
